new_terms.context: Refers to the core disruptive capability of AI agents to autonomously handle information-based tasks, extending beyond coding to the broader information economy.
A new product name from Anthropic, described as 'Claude Code for general computing,' designed to automate general information work like creating spreadsheets and drafting reports.
A key metric measuring how long an AI agent can work on a task autonomously before failing. The text states this is doubling every 4-7 months, unlocking more complex automation.
A highly compelling application that drives mainstream adoption of a technology. Coding is presented as the initial killer use case for AI agents.
A business term for the total revenue opportunity. The text argues that agentic AI expands the TAM beyond just LLMs to all information work.
An organization or project cited as the source for data on the growth of autonomous task horizons for AI agents.
An undefined abbreviation for a component in Anthropic's agent architecture, mentioned in the list: 'Claude Agent SDK, MCP, sub-agents'.
A component in an agentic architecture, presumably a specialized agent that performs sub-tasks under a main controller or framework.
